subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2 to 4 players.
- **Setup scaling** — VP chip pool and phase card availability scale by player count.
- **Solo mode** — Not included in the base game.

#### Cards
- **Game cards** — 114 cards total (worlds and developments).
- **Starting worlds** — 5 starting world cards (1 per player, 1 extra).
- **Action cards** — 4 sets of 7 action/phase cards (one set per player).

#### Tokens
- **VP chips** — VP chip pool (12 per player): 1-point and 5-point chips.
- **Goods tokens** — Cards placed face down on worlds represent produced goods.

#### Card Types
- **Developments** — Diamond-shaped cost icon; pay by discarding cards from hand.
- **Worlds (non-military)** — Circle cost icon; pay by discarding cards from hand.
- **Military worlds** — Red circle with number; require military strength instead of card payment.
- **Production worlds** — Colored halo; produce goods during Produce phase.
- **Windfall worlds** — Colored halo with outline; receive one good when settled, then only via special abilities.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
1. **VP chip pool** — Place VP chips in the center: 12 chips per player.
2. **Draw deck** — Shuffle all non-starting game cards into a face-down draw deck.
3. **Discard pile** — Designate a discard area.
4. **Phase summary** — Place the phase summary card where all players can see it.
5. **Player areas** — Each player needs space for their tableau (played cards), hand, and action cards.

--- 2 Players ---
- **VP pool** — 24 VP chips total.
- **Phase selection** — Each player selects 1 action card per round.

--- 3 Players ---
- **VP pool** — 36 VP chips total.

--- 4 Players ---
- **VP pool** — 48 VP chips total.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Action cards** — Each player takes 1 set of 7 action cards.
- **Starting world** — Deal 1 random starting world to each player; place it face up as the first card in your tableau.
- **Starting hand** — Deal 6 cards from the draw deck to each player.
- **Discard 2** — Each player discards 2 cards, keeping 4 in hand.
- **Cards as currency** — Cards in hand serve as both potential tableau cards and payment currency.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Beginning Play
- **Simultaneous selection** — All players simultaneously select 1 action card face down.
- **Reveal** — All selected action cards are revealed simultaneously.
- **Active phases** — Only the phases that were selected by at least one player are carried out.
- **Phase order** — Resolve phases in numerical order: I (Explore), II (Develop), III (Settle), IV (Consume), V (Produce).
- **First round** — Begin the first round immediately after setup.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Select phase** — Each player secretly chooses 1 of 7 action cards and places it face down.
2. **Reveal** — All chosen cards are revealed simultaneously.
3. **Resolve phases** — Execute only the chosen phases in order (I through V).
4. **Reclaim** — Players take back their action cards.
5. **Repeat** — Begin a new round until an end condition is met.

#### Phase Order
- **Phase I: Explore** — Draw cards from the deck, keep some.
- **Phase II: Develop** — Play a development card by discarding cards.
- **Phase III: Settle** — Play a world card by discarding cards or using military.
- **Phase IV: Consume** — Use consume powers to trade goods for cards or convert goods to VP chips.
- **Phase V: Produce** — Place goods on production worlds.

#### Chooser Bonus
- **Privilege** — The player who selected a phase gets an enhanced bonus for that phase.
- **Everyone participates** — All players perform each active phase, not just the chooser.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Phase I: Explore
- **Base action** — Draw 2 cards, keep 1.
- **Explore +5 bonus** — Draw 7 cards, keep 1 (chooser of Explore +5).
- **Explore +1,+1 bonus** — Draw 3 cards, keep 2 (chooser of Explore +1,+1).

#### Phase II: Develop
- **Base action** — Play 1 development from hand; pay its cost by discarding that many cards from hand.
- **Chooser bonus** — Pay 1 fewer card when developing.

#### Phase III: Settle
- **Non-military world** — Play 1 world from hand; pay its cost by discarding that many cards.
- **Military world** — Play 1 military world if your total military strength meets or exceeds the world's defense.
- **Military cost** — Military worlds cost no cards; you must simply have enough military icons in your tableau.
- **Chooser bonus** — Draw 1 card after settling.

#### Phase IV: Consume
- **Consume powers** — Activate consume powers on your tableau cards. Each power must consume specific good types.
- **VP conversion** — Most consume powers trade goods for VP chips.
- **Trade option** — Chooser of Consume: Trade sells 1 good for cards (draw cards equal to the good's trade value) before consuming.
- **Consume: 2x VP** — Chooser of Consume: 2x VP doubles all VP chips earned this phase.
- **Mandatory** — All goods must be consumed if consume powers are available.

#### Phase V: Produce
- **Production** — Place 1 good (face-down card from deck) on each of your production worlds that has no good.
- **Windfall exception** — Windfall worlds do not produce during normal Produce.
- **Chooser bonus** — Chooser may produce on 1 windfall world as well.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Cards as Currency
- **Dual purpose** — Every card can be played into your tableau or discarded as payment for another card.
- **Hand management** — Deciding what to keep and what to spend is the central tension.
- **No money** — There is no separate currency; cards are the only payment method.

#### Military Strength
- **Cumulative** — Military strength is the sum of all military icons across your tableau.
- **Free placement** — Military worlds are placed without discarding cards if you meet the strength requirement.
- **Temporary military** — Some cards provide temporary military boosts for a single settle action.

#### Simultaneous Play
- **No turn order** — All players act simultaneously within each phase.
- **Hidden selection** — Phase selection is secret, creating a guessing/reading metagame.
- **Phase economy** — Selecting a phase your opponents also want gives them free benefit.

#### Six-Cost Developments
- **Endgame scoring** — 6-cost developments have special endgame VP bonuses based on tableau composition.
- **Expensive** — Cost 6 cards to play (5 with chooser bonus), making them a major investment.
- **Examples** — Score bonus VP for military worlds, production worlds, specific good types, etc.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Triggers (either one)
- **Tableau size** — Any player places their 12th card in their tableau.
- **VP pool empty** — The VP chip pool runs out.

#### Final Round
- **Complete the round** — Finish the current round through all active phases.
- **VP chips can go negative** — If the pool empties mid-phase, use substitutes; no VP is lost.

#### Scoring
- **Tableau card VP** — Sum VP printed on all developments and worlds in your tableau.
- **VP chips** — Add all VP chips accumulated during the game.
- **6-cost development bonuses** — Calculate endgame bonus VP from any 6-cost developments.
- **Total** — Sum all sources; highest total wins.

#### Tiebreaker
- **Cards plus goods** — Player with the most cards in hand plus goods on worlds wins ties.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Read your starting world** — Your starting world dictates your early strategy (military, production, or development focus).
- **Plan a consume engine** — Producing and consuming goods for VP chips is the most reliable scoring path.
- **Keep flexible cards** — Early on, keep cards that work in multiple strategies rather than committing too hard.
- **Watch opponents** — Predict which phases opponents will choose to get free actions.
- **Phase selection efficiency** — Choose phases that benefit you more than your opponents.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Overcommitting to military** — Military lets you settle for free but does not generate VP on its own.
- **Ignoring consume powers** — Without consume abilities, produced goods sit unused and score nothing.
- **Spending key cards as payment** — Discarding a card you need to play later is a common regret.
- **Neglecting 6-cost developments** — These provide massive endgame bonuses but require early planning.
- **Phase selection without reading the table** — Choosing Produce when no one has goods wastes an opportunity.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Produce/Consume vs Develop/Settle
- **Produce/Consume loop** — Generates steady VP chips each round.
- **Develop/Settle rush** — Races to 12 tableau cards to end the game on your terms.

#### Decision 2: Military vs Peaceful Settling
- **Military** — Saves cards when settling but requires military infrastructure.
- **Peaceful** — Costs more cards but gives more flexibility in world choice.

#### Decision 3: When to Trigger Endgame
- **End early** — If your engine is ahead, rush to 12 cards or deplete the VP pool.
- **Stall** — If behind, avoid triggering end conditions and build toward big late-game scoring.
